objective: Apply a fork-only runtime correction to remove nonexistent stable vLLM 0.16.1 references, default this image path to a pinned nightly build that supports Qwen3.5 language_model_only startup, and keep deterministic stable override support at vLLM 0.16.0.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to Dockerfile, docker-bake.hcl, src/engine_args.py, scripts/config_doctor.py, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Default image path pins nightly vLLM to 0.16.1rc1.dev257+g3b23d57c9 (deterministic) so Qwen3.5 text-only path (`LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY=true`) is available.
Default nightly path pins transformers to immutable `TRANSFORMERS_REF=421c7f6248e28d24d84ee000252a1e71fbc24917` and exposes this as an explicit Docker/buildx override.
Build configuration keeps explicit stable override at VLLM_VERSION=0.16.0 when VLLM_NIGHTLY=false.
Engine arg parsing fails fast with an actionable error when Qwen3.5 is selected but runtime lacks language_model_only support or it is disabled.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this correction commit. Operational fast rollback without code revert is to build/deploy with `VLLM_NIGHTLY=false` and `VLLM_VERSION=0.16.0`, or keep nightly enabled while reverting only `TRANSFORMERS_REF` to a previously validated commit SHA, then switch production presets away from `Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B` (or disable LANGUAGE_MODEL_ONLY-dependent paths) until a compatible nightly image is redeployed. Endpoint-level rollback remains pinning to the last known-good fork tag while rebuild/redeploy completes.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are runtime behavior changes from defaulting to nightly and false-positive startup blocks from stricter Qwen3.5 validation. Mitigation: pin exact nightly version (`0.16.1rc1.dev257+g3b23d57c9`), keep explicit stable override (`0.16.0`) for deterministic fallback, keep code changes narrow to messaging/compatibility guards, and require staged runtime validation.
